theory.intro: To compare lengths fairly, first write them in the **same unit**. Then the numbers can be compared directly.
facts: You cannot compare $1.2$ m and $90$ cm until both use one unit.
Useful links: $1$ m $= 100$ cm, $1$ cm $= 10$ mm, $1$ km $= 1000$ m.
Convert to the **smaller** unit to avoid decimals, or to a common unit of your choice.
steps: Choose one common unit for all the lengths.
Convert every length into that unit.
